subject: English
source: Aruvi static lookup. Derived from NCF Languages CG (Section 2.4.1.3) and a 6-chapter mapping audit (IX Ch 3, IX Ch 7) showing 85% C-code overlap across chapters. The 6-spine itself carries NCF compliance — per-chapter mapping is redundant.

reading_for_comprehension.label: Reading for Comprehension
reading_for_comprehension.notes: Layered reading at secondary: meaning-making, close-reading critique, reasoning over text, and exposure to multilingual/multicultural reading.

speaking.label: Speaking
speaking.notes: Debate, expressing agreement/disagreement with reasons, arguing with rationale.

writing.label: Writing
writing.notes: Different writing styles (narrative/descriptive/expository/persuasive), real-life writing (condolence, notice, advertisement, slogan), media scripting, and composing literary texts. The chapter's task_type tag selects the dominant code.

vocabulary_grammar.label: Vocabulary and Grammar
vocabulary_grammar.notes: At secondary, grammar/vocabulary work is largely embedded inside close-reading style analysis (C-2.2) rather than as a stand-alone CG.

beyond_text.label: Beyond the Text
beyond_text.notes: Multilingual heritage exploration, cultural connections, identity/language, and geographic distribution of languages — typically delivered as projects, interviews, comparative studies.
